steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: The coefficients $11$, $-11$, and $77$ all share a factor of $11$. Every term contains at least one $x$. So the GCF is $11x$.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $x(10x^{10}-10x^{9}+76x)$ — expands to $10x^{11}-10x^{10}+76x^{2}$, wrong degrees. Eliminate.
  • B) $x(11^{10}-11^{9}+77)$ — constants only inside, gives a single $x$ term, not a polynomial of degree 10. Eliminate.
  • C) $11x(x^{10}-x^{9}+7x)$ — expands to $11x^{11}-11x^{10}+77x^{2}$, wrong degrees. Eliminate.
  • D) $11x(x^{9}-x^{8}+7)$ — expands to $11x^{10}-11x^{9}+77x$. Matches.
D is correct.
